questions.question: What is the name of the mobile application launched by the HRD Ministry, which provides mock tests for exams like JEE and NEET?
questions.explanation: Union Human Resource Development Minister Ramesh Pokhriyal Nishank launched a new mobile application named the ‘National Test Abhyas’. The application enables the students to take up high-quality mock tests for the upcoming exams that come under the National Testing Agency’s purview including JEE Main and NEET. It has been developed by the National Testing Agency-NTA. The tests can be downloaded without any fee.

questions.question: What is the name of first agri-futures index of India, to be launched by the National Commodity & Derivatives Exchange Limited (NCDEX)?
questions.explanation: National Commodity & Derivatives Exchange Limited (NCDEX) is an online multi-commodity exchange, headquartered at Mumbai, India. NCDEX was recently in news as the exchange announced that it will launch the country’s first agri-futures index, NCDEX AGRIDEX. The exchange said it has already begun the mock trading in agri-index futures contracts. AGRIDEX represents a basket of ten liquid agricultural commodities on the NCDEX and will serve as a benchmark for India’s strong agricultural sector.

questions.question: What is the theme of ‘World Bee Day 2020’?
questions.explanation: The United Nations has been celebrating the World Bee Day on May 20, every year, to raise awareness of the importance of pollinators. The day also highlights the threats faced by pollinators and their contribution to sustainable development. This year, World Bee Day focusses on bee production and practices adopted by beekeepers to support their livelihoods. A virtual event was planned for celebrating Bee day under theme “Bee Engaged”. This will highlight the importance of traditional knowledge related to beekeeping and the use of bee-derived products and services.

questions.question: Who is set to take charge as the chairman of the World Health Organization (WHO) Executive Board from 2020?
questions.explanation: Union Health Minister Harsh Vardhan is set to take charge as the chairman of the World Health Organization (WHO) Executive Board. Harsh Vardhan would succeed the current chairman of WHO Executive Board, Dr Hiroki Nakatani of Japan. The Executive Board of WHO comprises 34 executives related to the field of health, each representing a member country. The Chairman’s post is held by rotation among the regional groups.

questions.question: The Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS), that was recently in news is associated with providing credit for which sector?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet has approved an additional funding of up to Rs 3 lakh crore through the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) for the MSME sector. The Micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) that are hit hard by the coronavirus crisis will be granted an emergency credit at the rate of 9.25 per cent. 100 per cent guarantee coverage will be provided by National Credit Guarantee Trustee Company (NCGTC) for eligible MSMEs and MUDRA borrowers, in the form of a guaranteed emergency credit line (GECL) facility.

questions.question: India recently signed an addendum with which country to operationalise five new ports of call, to enhance trade and connectivity?
questions.explanation: High Commissioner of India to Bangladesh signed an addendum to the ‘Protocol on Inland Water Transit and Trade’ with the Shipping Ministry of Bangladesh, in Dhaka. It was signed to operationalise five new ports of call and two more protocol routes to enhance trade and connectivity between the two countries. The number of Indo-Bangladesh Protocol (IBP) routes are also being increased from 8 to 10. Among the newly added ports of call, Dhulian, Maia, Kolaghat, Sonamura and Jogigopha are in India while Rajshahi, Sultanganj, Chilmari, Daudkandi and Bahadurabad are in Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which famous platform has introduced a feature called ‘bedtime reminder’, as a part of its wellness and screen time tools?
questions.explanation: YouTube has recently has introduced a feature called ‘bedtime reminder’, which will enable the user to cut down his screen time without compromising their sleep. This new feature has been rolled out as a part of YouTube’s wellness and screen time tools. It was released during Google’s Digital Wellbeing initiative. The feature enables the user to set a reminder at a specific time to stop watching videos and go to bed. The ‘Take a Break’ feature that lets users to set reminders to take a break from watching videos.

questions.question: Which year has marked the first official observance of the International Tea Day?
questions.explanation: The First Observance of the International Tea Day under the theme ‘Harnessing benefits for all from field to cup’ was held on 21 May 2020. The Day brings together the countries where tea cultivation is an important source of jobs and incomes. Last year, the United Nations General Assembly has designated May 21 as International Tea Day on the basis of the proposal moved by India at the FAO Intergovernmental Group (IGG). The first International Tea Day was celebrated virtually and was attended by FAO head, QU Dongyu

questions.question: GFR has been in news for amendments related to procurement of the Government. What does GFR stand for?
questions.explanation: The Union Government has notified amendments to General Financial Rules (GFR) 2017, to make goods and services valued less than Rs 200 crore are procured from domestic firms. As announced in the recent economic stimulus package, global procurements will be disallowed up to Rs 200 Crore, which will reduce unfair competition among the domestic suppliers and MSMEs. GFR is amended such that the limit of procurement may be prescribed by the Department of Expenditure from time to time.

questions.question: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) partnered with which public sector enterprise to set up a joint venture company for renewable energy business?
questions.explanation: Leading Public Sector Enterprises Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) and NTPC Ltd signed a Memorandum of understanding (MoU), to set up a joint venture company for the renewable energy business. As per the MoU, NTPC and ONGC will explore and set up renewable power projects including offshore wind in India and in other countries. The enterprises will also roll-out their expertise in the fields of sustainability, storage, e-mobility and ESG (Environmental, Social and Governance) compliant projects.
